title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
pitch: A concierge and his lobby boy get tangled in art theft, murder, and pastry. Peak Wes Anderson.

title: Parasite
pitch: A poor family schemes their way into a rich household. Then the basement opens.

title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
pitch: A laundromat owner discovers she can access every version of herself across the multiverse. Chaotic, emotional, brilliant.

title: In Bruges
pitch: Two hitmen hide out in Bruges and hate everything about it. Profane, dark, unexpectedly moving.

title: The Shawshank Redemption
pitch: A wrongly imprisoned banker slowly, quietly engineers the most satisfying escape in cinema.

title: Jojo Rabbit
pitch: A Nazi boy discovers his mother is hiding a Jewish girl. His imaginary friend is Hitler. It works.

title: Arrival
pitch: A linguist tries to communicate with aliens. The twist reframes the entire film — and how you think about time.

title: The Banshees of Inisherin
pitch: A man's best friend suddenly stops talking to him on a small Irish island. Funnier and darker than it sounds.

title: Mad Max: Fury Road
pitch: A two-hour car chase across a post-apocalyptic desert. The most beautiful action film ever made.

title: The Big Lebowski
pitch: The Dude's rug gets peed on. Everything escalates from there. The Coen brothers at their most absurd.

title: Whiplash
pitch: A jazz drummer and his abusive teacher wage psychological war. You'll feel your pulse in your fingertips.

title: Knives Out
pitch: A wealthy patriarch dies, every family member is suspect, and Daniel Craig has a Southern accent. Pure fun.

title: Moonlight
pitch: Three chapters of a Black man's life in Miami. Quiet, devastating, luminous.

title: Hunt for the Wilderpeople
pitch: A foster kid and his grumpy uncle go on the run in the New Zealand bush. Taika Waititi's warmest film.

title: Inception
pitch: Thieves who steal secrets from dreams attempt to plant an idea instead. Dreams within dreams within dreams.

title: The Farewell
pitch: A Chinese-American family stages a wedding to say goodbye to their dying grandmother — without telling her.

title: Superbad
pitch: Two high school friends try to buy alcohol for a party before graduation. Still the gold standard for teen comedy.

title: No Country for Old Men
pitch: A hunter finds drug money in the desert. The man coming after him has a cattle bolt. Best villain ever committed to film.

title: Amélie
pitch: A shy Parisian waitress orchestrates small miracles for the people around her. Pure cinematic joy.

title: The Social Network
pitch: The founding of Facebook as a story about ambition, betrayal, and loneliness. Sorkin's sharpest script.

title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
pitch: A painter falls in love with the woman she's been commissioned to paint. Every frame is a painting itself.

title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
pitch: Miles Morales meets every Spider-Person from every universe. The most visually inventive animated film in years.

title: Zodiac
pitch: The Zodiac killer investigation consumes everyone who touches it. Fincher's most obsessive film about obsession.

title: The Nice Guys
pitch: A PI and an enforcer investigate a missing girl in 1977 LA. Gosling and Crowe have perfect chemistry.

title: Interstellar
pitch: A farmer-astronaut searches for a new home for humanity. The docking scene. The bookshelf scene. That wave.

title: Lady Bird
pitch: A Sacramento teen fights with her mom, falls in love badly, and figures out who she is. Greta Gerwig's debut.

title: Sicario
pitch: An FBI agent gets pulled into the shadow war on the Mexican border. The tunnel sequence is cinema at its tensest.

title: The Intouchables
pitch: A wealthy quadriplegic hires a charismatic ex-con as his caretaker. French feel-good done right.

title: Ex Machina
pitch: A programmer is invited to test whether an AI is truly conscious. The AI has other plans.

title: The Truman Show
pitch: A man's entire life is a TV show and he doesn't know it. Jim Carrey's finest dramatic performance.

title: Capernaum
pitch: A 12-year-old Lebanese boy sues his parents for giving him life. Gut-wrenching and unforgettable.

title: Game Night
pitch: A murder mystery party becomes an actual kidnapping. Nobody can tell the difference. Smarter than it has any right to be.

title: Dune
pitch: Desert planet, giant worms, political intrigue, a chosen one who maybe shouldn't be. Villeneuve makes it feel like a painting.

title: Marriage Story
pitch: A couple navigates divorce with love, lawyers, and the argument scene that will haunt you.

title: What We Do in the Shadows
pitch: Vampire flatmates in Wellington deal with chores, nightclub bouncers, and not being invited inside. A mockumentary masterpiece.

title: The Prestige
pitch: Two rival magicians destroy each other trying to create the ultimate illusion. Watch it twice.

title: Past Lives
pitch: Two childhood sweethearts from Seoul reconnect 24 years later in New York. The final scene is devastating.

title: Coco
pitch: A boy travels to the Land of the Dead to find his great-grandfather. You will cry. Everyone cries.

title: Prisoners
pitch: Two girls disappear. Hugh Jackman takes matters into his own hands. Villeneuve's most suffocating film.

title: The Lunchbox
pitch: A mistaken lunch delivery in Mumbai starts a correspondence between two lonely people. Gentle and lovely.

title: Drive
pitch: A stunt driver moonlights as a getaway driver. Ryan Gosling says twelve words. Every shot is neon-lit perfection.

title: The Worst Person in the World
pitch: A young woman in Oslo tries to figure out what she wants from life and love. The most relatable existential crisis on film.

title: Ratatouille
pitch: A rat wants to be a chef in Paris. The food critic scene alone justifies Pixar's existence.

title: Nightcrawler
pitch: A sociopath becomes a freelance crime journalist in LA. Jake Gyllenhaal is terrifying because he never blinks.

title: Paddington 2
pitch: A bear tries to buy a pop-up book. Hugh Grant plays a washed-up actor villain. The highest-rated film on Rotten Tomatoes for a reason.

title: Wind River
pitch: A murder investigation on a Native American reservation in Wyoming winter. Written and directed by the Sicario screenwriter.

title: The Lobster
pitch: In a dystopia, single people must find a partner in 45 days or be turned into an animal. Absurdist and oddly romantic.

title: Barbie
pitch: Barbie has an existential crisis, visits the real world, and discovers patriarchy. Way smarter than it needed to be.

title: Soul
pitch: A jazz musician dies and has to figure out the meaning of life before getting his body back. Pixar for adults.

title: Oppenheimer
pitch: The man who built the bomb and the world that judged him for it. Three hours that feel like a detonation.

title: The Godfather
pitch: A family business — if the business is organised crime. The film against which all others are measured.

title: Pulp Fiction
pitch: Interlocking crime stories told out of order. A burger has never been more menacing.

title: Fight Club
pitch: An insomniac and a soap salesman start an underground fight club. The first rule is the first rule.

title: Spirited Away
pitch: A girl stumbles into a spirit world bathhouse. Miyazaki's magnum opus. Every frame is a painting.

title: The Matrix
pitch: A hacker discovers reality is a simulation. Changed action cinema and philosophy classes forever.

title: Get Out
pitch: A Black man visits his white girlfriend's family. The teacup scene. Jordan Peele's perfect debut.

title: Her
pitch: A man falls in love with his AI assistant. More romantic and less weird than it sounds.

title: Blade Runner 2049
pitch: A replicant cop uncovers a secret that could unravel society. Even more gorgeous than the original.

title: The Princess Bride
pitch: Fencing, fighting, torture, revenge, giants, monsters, chases, escapes, true love, miracles.

title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
pitch: A couple erases each other from memory. Then meets again. The saddest love story ever told.

title: Goodfellas
pitch: Henry Hill's rise and fall in the mob. The tracking shot through the kitchen is cinema history.

title: Hot Fuzz
pitch: A London supercop transfers to a sleepy village. Then people start dying. Edgar Wright's best.

title: Memento
pitch: A man with no short-term memory hunts his wife's killer. Told backwards. Nolan's cleverest puzzle.

title: Lost in Translation
pitch: Two Americans share sleepless nights in Tokyo. What they whisper at the end doesn't matter.

title: The Departed
pitch: A cop infiltrates the mob. A mobster infiltrates the police. Everyone's paranoid. Everyone should be.

title: Bridesmaids
pitch: A maid of honour slowly spirals while planning her best friend's wedding. The food poisoning scene.

title: Fargo
pitch: A husband hires two criminals to kidnap his wife. In Minnesota. Nothing goes right. Oh ya, you betcha.

title: 12 Angry Men
pitch: Twelve jurors debate a murder case in one room. One dissents. The greatest courtroom drama ever made.

title: Before Sunrise
pitch: Two strangers walk and talk through Vienna for one night. Proof that conversation can be cinema.

title: District 9
pitch: Aliens arrive in Johannesburg — as refugees. A bureaucrat gets too close. Apartheid as sci-fi.

title: The Iron Giant
pitch: A boy befriends a giant robot during the Cold War. 'Superman.' If you know, you're already crying.

title: Call Me By Your Name
pitch: A summer in Italy. A scholar. A peach. Michael Stuhlbarg's monologue is the best father-son scene in cinema.

title: Gone Girl
pitch: On their anniversary, a man's wife disappears. Then her diary surfaces. Fincher's sharpest knife.

title: Palm Springs
pitch: Two wedding guests get stuck in a time loop. The best pandemic comfort film, made before the pandemic.

title: Spotlight
pitch: Boston Globe journalists investigate the Catholic Church abuse scandal. Restrained, relentless, infuriating.

title: Baby Driver
pitch: A getaway driver soundtracks his life. Every car chase choreographed to music. Edgar Wright goes fast.

title: The Death of Stalin
pitch: Soviet ministers scramble for power after Stalin drops dead. Somehow both hilarious and horrifying.

title: Annihilation
pitch: Five scientists enter a shimmering anomaly. What's inside defies explanation. The bear scene.

title: Manchester by the Sea
pitch: A man returns to his hometown after tragedy. Casey Affleck carries the weight of the world in silence.

title: John Wick
pitch: A retired assassin comes back because someone killed his dog. 77 people regret that decision.

title: Booksmart
pitch: Two overachieving best friends try to cram four years of partying into one night. A teen comedy for the ages.

title: Howl's Moving Castle
pitch: A cursed girl moves into a wizard's walking castle. Miyazaki, romance, and a fire demon voiced by Billy Crystal.

title: Se7en
pitch: Two detectives track a serial killer using the seven deadly sins. What's in the box remains cinema's cruellest question.

title: Kill Bill: Volume 1
pitch: A bride wakes from a coma and hunts down her former assassination squad. Tarantino's most stylish revenge.

title: Nomadland
pitch: A woman lives in a van across the American West. Frances McDormand makes loneliness look like freedom.

title: Little Miss Sunshine
pitch: A dysfunctional family road-trips to a beauty pageant in a broken VW bus. The dance routine at the end.

title: Inside Out
pitch: The emotions inside an 11-year-old's head navigate her move to a new city. Pixar made everyone cry. Again.

title: The Silence of the Lambs
pitch: An FBI trainee consults an imprisoned cannibal to catch another killer. Hopkins has twelve minutes of screen time. It's enough.

title: Moon
pitch: A man alone on a lunar base starts to suspect his employer. Sam Rockwell plays against himself. Literally.

title: Pride & Prejudice
pitch: Elizabeth Bennet and Mr Darcy spar across the English countryside. The hand-flex scene launched a thousand rewatches.

title: The Raid
pitch: A SWAT team fights floor by floor through a drug lord's apartment block. The best martial arts film of the century.

title: Good Will Hunting
pitch: A janitor at MIT solves impossible math problems. Robin Williams makes you cry in a park. 'It's not your fault.'

title: Klaus
pitch: A selfish postman discovers the origin of Santa Claus. Netflix's first animated film, and it's gorgeous.

title: Hereditary
pitch: A family unravels after their grandmother dies. The car scene. You'll know it when you see it.

title: Edge of Tomorrow
pitch: A soldier relives the same alien invasion over and over. Groundhog Day meets Saving Private Ryan.

title: The Lives of Others
pitch: A Stasi agent surveils a playwright in 1984 East Berlin. Slowly, the watcher becomes human.

title: Shaun of the Dead
pitch: A man tries to win back his girlfriend during a zombie apocalypse. By going to the pub.

title: Gravity
pitch: An astronaut is stranded in orbit after debris destroys everything. 91 minutes of pure survival tension.

title: Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri
pitch: A mother rents billboards to shame the police into solving her daughter's murder. Frances McDormand is a force of nature.

title: Midsommar
pitch: A couple attends a Swedish midsummer festival. The sunshine makes the horror worse, not better.

title: About Time
pitch: A man can time travel but only uses it to perfect his love life. Surprisingly devastating by the end.

title: Oldboy
pitch: A man is imprisoned for 15 years with no explanation, then released. The twist will haunt you forever.

title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty
pitch: A daydreamer goes on an actual adventure for once. The skateboarding-in-Iceland scene alone is worth it.

title: Uncut Gems
pitch: Adam Sandler as a gambling-addicted jeweler who cannot stop making terrible bets. Your blood pressure will spike.

title: La La Land
pitch: Two dreamers fall in love in LA. The ending is either perfect or heartbreaking depending on who you ask.

title: The Florida Project
pitch: A six-year-old has the best summer ever in a motel near Disney World. The adults around her are barely surviving.

title: Airplane!
pitch: A disaster movie parody with more jokes per minute than any film ever made. Don't call me Shirley.

title: Gladiator
pitch: A Roman general becomes a slave becomes a gladiator. Russell Crowe will make you believe in vengeance.

title: Toy Story
pitch: Your toys are alive and have feelings. Pixar invented an entire genre with this one.

title: The Witch
pitch: A Puritan family in 1630s New England suspects witchcraft. The goat knows something.

title: Bo Burnham: Inside
pitch: A comedian alone in a room during lockdown makes a special about the internet. Funnier and sadder than it should be.

title: My Neighbor Totoro
pitch: Two sisters befriend a giant forest spirit. Studio Ghibli at its most purely comforting.

title: A Separation
pitch: An Iranian couple's divorce gets complicated by a caretaker's accusation. Every character is right and wrong.

title: Once
pitch: A street musician and a Czech immigrant make an album together. The songs are so good they won an Oscar.

title: The Thing
pitch: An alien imitates anyone it touches at an Antarctic research station. Trust nobody, especially the dog.

title: Eighth Grade
pitch: The last week of eighth grade for a shy girl who makes YouTube videos nobody watches. Painfully relatable.

title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout
pitch: Tom Cruise does his own stunts including a HALO jump and a helicopter chase. The man is uninsurable.

title: Fantastic Mr. Fox
pitch: Wes Anderson makes a stop-motion film about a fox who can't stop stealing chickens. Cussing fantastic.

title: Mulholland Drive
pitch: A woman with amnesia and an aspiring actress investigate a mystery in Hollywood. David Lynch at his Lynchiest.

title: Legally Blonde
pitch: A sorority girl gets into Harvard Law to win back her ex. Proves everyone wrong while being unapologetically herself.

title: The Handmaiden
pitch: A pickpocket is hired to help swindle a Japanese heiress in 1930s Korea. Every twist rewrites the whole story.

title: Chef
pitch: A chef quits his fancy restaurant to run a food truck. You will be ordering Cuban sandwiches within the hour.

title: Green Book
pitch: A bouncer drives a Black pianist through the 1960s South. Viggo Mortensen eats an entire pizza by folding it in half.
Two soldiers must cross enemy lines to deliver a message. Shot to look like one continuous take.

title: Minari
pitch: A Korean-American family starts a farm in Arkansas. The grandmother steals every scene she's in.

title: Deadpool
pitch: A mercenary gets superpowers and breaks the fourth wall constantly. Ryan Reynolds was born for this role.

title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
pitch: A nobody pretends to be somebody in 1950s Italy. Matt Damon has never been more unsettling.

title: Up
pitch: An old man flies his house to South America with balloons. The first ten minutes will wreck you emotionally.

title: Blade Runner
pitch: A detective hunts rogue androids in a rainy cyberpunk LA. Rutger Hauer's final monologue is cinema perfection.

title: Catch Me If You Can
pitch: A teenager impersonates a pilot, doctor, and lawyer while the FBI chases him. Based on a true story, incredibly.

title: There Will Be Blood
pitch: An oil prospector's greed consumes everything around him. Daniel Day-Lewis drinks your milkshake.

title: Scott Pilgrim vs. the World
pitch: A slacker must defeat his new girlfriend's seven evil exes in video game-style battles. Edgar Wright at maximum fun.

title: Room
pitch: A mother and her five-year-old son escape captivity. Brie Larson won an Oscar. You will cry.

title: Triangle of Sadness
pitch: Influencers and billionaires on a luxury yacht get a harsh reality check. The seasick dinner scene is legendary.

title: Tár
pitch: Cate Blanchett plays a world-renowned conductor whose past catches up with her. A masterclass in slow-burn tension.

title: Top Gun: Maverick
pitch: Tom Cruise returns to the danger zone 36 years later. A legacy sequel that's somehow better than the original.

title: Glass Onion
pitch: Detective Blanc investigates a murder mystery on a tech billionaire's private island. The onion has many layers.

title: The Whale
pitch: A reclusive English teacher tries to reconnect with his estranged daughter. Brendan Fraser's comeback role.

title: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
pitch: A tiny shell with shoes searches for his family through a documentary crew. The most wholesome film ever made.

title: Nope
pitch: Siblings on a horse ranch discover something terrifying in the sky. Jordan Peele turns spectacle itself into horror.

title: The Menu
pitch: A celebrity chef serves a very exclusive, very deadly tasting menu. Anya Taylor-Joy orders a cheeseburger.

title: RRR
pitch: Two Indian revolutionaries team up against the British Empire. The most spectacularly over-the-top action film ever made.

title: Aftersun
pitch: A woman revisits memories of a holiday with her father when she was eleven. Quietly devastating.

title: Decision to Leave
pitch: A detective falls for the prime suspect in a murder case. Park Chan-wook crafts a hypnotic love story.

title: Pig
pitch: Nicolas Cage's truffle-hunting pig is stolen. This is not the action movie you expect. It's better.

title: The Mitchells vs. the Machines
pitch: A dysfunctional family road trip becomes humanity's last hope against a robot apocalypse. Wildly creative animation.

title: Prey
pitch: A Comanche warrior faces the Predator in 1719. The best Predator sequel by a mile.

title: Emily the Criminal
pitch: A woman buried in student debt starts running credit card scams. Aubrey Plaza proves she can do anything.

title: Puss in Boots: The Last Wish
pitch: Puss in Boots faces mortality and it's somehow the best animated film in years. The wolf is genuinely terrifying.

title: The Banshees of Inisherin
pitch: A man's best friend suddenly decides he doesn't like him anymore. A breakup story set on an Irish island.

title: Challengers
pitch: A love triangle between tennis players told backwards through a championship match. Luca Guadagnino at his most electric.

title: The Holdovers
pitch: A grumpy classics teacher and a troubled student are stuck at boarding school over Christmas 1970. Quietly devastating.

title: Borat
pitch: A Kazakh journalist travels across America. What he exposes about his hosts is funnier than anything he does.

title: Poor Things
pitch: A reanimated woman discovers the world with the curiosity of a child and the freedom of someone with nothing to lose.

title: Tucker and Dale vs Evil
pitch: Two hillbillies are mistaken for murderers by clueless college kids. Every horror trope flipped on its head.

title: The Zone of Interest
pitch: The commandant of Auschwitz and his family live an idyllic life next door to the camp. Horror through absence.

title: Step Brothers
pitch: Two middle-aged men become stepbrothers and act like children. Stupid in the most brilliant way possible.

title: Killers of the Flower Moon
pitch: The systematic murder of Osage Nation members for oil money in 1920s Oklahoma. Scorsese, DiCaprio, De Niro. A slow burn that sears.

title: Anatomy of a Fall
pitch: A woman is accused of killing her husband. The trial dissects their marriage. You'll argue about the verdict for days.

title: The Hangover
pitch: Three groomsmen wake up in Vegas with no memory, a tiger, and a missing groom. The blueprint for modern comedy.

title: Dunkirk
pitch: The evacuation of Dunkirk told from land, sea, and air simultaneously. Almost no dialogue, all tension.

title: Saltburn
pitch: An Oxford student insinuates himself into an aristocratic family's country estate. Seductive and deeply unsettling.

title: The Revenant
pitch: A frontiersman is left for dead after a bear attack and crawls through the wilderness for revenge. Raw survival cinema.

title: The Master
pitch: A WWII veteran falls under the spell of a charismatic cult leader. Hoffman and Phoenix in a psychological cage match.

title: Eurovision Song Contest: The Story of Fire Saga
pitch: An Icelandic duo enters Eurovision. Will Ferrell sings with passion. The song-along scene is genuine magic.

title: Spy
pitch: A CIA desk analyst goes undercover as a spy. Melissa McCarthy and Jason Statham are comedy gold together.

title: The Darjeeling Limited
pitch: Three brothers take a train across India to find their mother. Peak Wes Anderson melancholy disguised as a travelogue.

title: Being John Malkovich
pitch: A puppeteer discovers a portal into John Malkovich's brain. Charlie Kaufman's brain is weirder than any sci-fi.

title: Tropic Thunder
pitch: Actors filming a war movie accidentally stumble into a real conflict. Tom Cruise's cameo alone is worth it.

title: Kiki's Delivery Service
pitch: A young witch moves to a new city and starts a delivery service on her broomstick. Pure Ghibli comfort.

title: American Fiction
pitch: A Black novelist writes a satirical book playing into stereotypes — and it becomes a bestseller. Razor-sharp on race and publishing.

title: The Great Escape
pitch: Allied POWs plan a massive escape from a German camp. Steve McQueen on a motorcycle. Old-school adventure at its finest.

title: The Gentlemen
pitch: A British drug lord tries to sell his empire. Guy Ritchie at his sharpest, Hugh Grant at his slimiest.

title: The Other Guys
pitch: Two mismatched NYPD detectives accidentally uncover a massive financial crime. Will Ferrell and Mark Wahlberg are a perfect pair.

title: WALL·E
pitch: A lonely robot on an abandoned Earth falls in love with a sleek probe. Almost no dialogue. All heart.

title: Encanto
pitch: A Colombian family all have magical powers — except one. The music is impossibly catchy and the family dynamics feel real.

title: Inside Out 2
pitch: Anxiety shows up in Riley's teenage brain and takes over. Pixar makes you cry about emotions having emotions. Again.

title: 21 Jump Street
pitch: Two cops go undercover at a high school and discover they're no longer cool. Channing Tatum's comedy awakening.

title: Apocalypto
pitch: A Mayan villager is captured for sacrifice and runs for his life through the jungle. Relentless, visceral, entirely in Yucatec Maya.

title: Conclave
pitch: Cardinals gather in the Vatican to elect a new pope. Political intrigue, secrets, and a final twist that reframes everything.

title: This Is Spinal Tap
pitch: A documentary crew follows Britain's loudest band on their disastrous American tour. These go to eleven.

title: Memoria
pitch: A woman in Colombia hears a mysterious bang that nobody else can hear. Apichatpong Weerasethakul's most hypnotic film.

title: The Batman
pitch: Batman as a detective noir. Gotham has never looked this rain-soaked or felt this corrupt. Pattinson nails the damaged vigilante.

title: Porco Rosso
pitch: A WWI flying ace has been cursed into a pig and now hunts air pirates over the Adriatic. Peak Ghibli vibes.

title: The Substance
pitch: An aging celebrity uses a black-market drug that creates a younger version of herself. Body horror meets Hollywood satire.

title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail
pitch: King Arthur and his knights quest for the Holy Grail. The Black Knight. The French taunters. The killer rabbit. Timeless.

title: School of Rock
pitch: A failing musician poses as a substitute teacher and turns his class into a rock band. Jack Black's most lovable performance.

title: Anora
pitch: A Brooklyn stripper marries the son of a Russian oligarch. Then his parents find out. Palme d'Or winner for a reason.

title: Burn After Reading
pitch: Gym employees find a CIA agent's memoir and try to blackmail him. Everyone is an idiot. The Coens' most cynical farce.

title: Tenet
pitch: Time moves forwards and backwards simultaneously. You won't fully understand it. You'll want to watch it again immediately.

title: The Wind Rises
pitch: The life of the engineer who designed Japan's WWII fighter planes. Miyazaki's most personal and morally complex film.

title: Mid90s
pitch: A 13-year-old finds belonging with a group of skaters in 90s LA. Jonah Hill's directorial debut, shot on 16mm. Nostalgic and tender.

title: Napoleon Dynamite
pitch: An awkward Idaho teenager helps his friend run for class president. The dance scene is cinema's greatest underdog moment.

title: Prisoners of the Ghostland
pitch: Nic Cage in a leather suit rigged with explosives in a post-apocalyptic Japan. It's exactly as unhinged as it sounds.

title: Sing Street
pitch: A Dublin teenager starts a band to impress a girl in 1980s Ireland. The songs are genuinely great. John Carney's best.

title: Never Let Me Go
pitch: Students at an idyllic English boarding school discover the horrifying purpose they were created for. Quiet devastation.

title: The Lighthouse
pitch: Two lighthouse keepers go mad on a remote island. Shot in black and white, square format. Dafoe and Pattinson screaming into the void.

title: Moonrise Kingdom
pitch: Two 12-year-olds run away together on a New England island. Wes Anderson's most tender film, somehow.

title: Bottoms
pitch: Two unpopular girls start a fight club to hook up with cheerleaders. Absurdist high school comedy that goes completely off the rails.

title: Civil War
pitch: War journalists cross a fractured America during a second civil war. Alex Garland makes you feel every bullet and moral compromise.

title: Whisper of the Heart
pitch: A bookish girl follows the trail of a mysterious boy through Tokyo's streets. Ghibli's most grounded and romantic coming-of-age.

title: Shoplifters
pitch: A family of petty thieves in Tokyo takes in an abused child. Hirokazu Kore-eda asks what makes a family real.

title: Dodgeball
pitch: A ragtag gym enters a dodgeball tournament to save their business. Ben Stiller's villain is peak absurdity.

title: I'm Still Here
pitch: A Brazilian woman's husband is taken by the military dictatorship in the 1970s. She refuses to be silent. Fernanda Torres is extraordinary.

title: Snatch
pitch: A stolen diamond, bare-knuckle boxing, and Brad Pitt speaking incomprehensible Irish. Guy Ritchie's tightest, funniest script.

title: Under the Skin
pitch: Scarlett Johansson drives around Scotland picking up men. She's not human. Nothing will prepare you for this film.

title: The Boy and the Heron
pitch: A grieving boy follows a mysterious heron into a fantastical world. Miyazaki's farewell film — strange, beautiful, deeply personal.
